rawMetadata: {"compiler":{"version":"0.8.30+commit.73712a01"},"language":"Solidity","output":{"abi":[{"inputs":[],"name":"SequencerDown","type":"error"},{"inputs":[],"name":"SequencerResyncIncomplete","type":"error"},{"inputs":[],"name":"getSequencerUptimeFeed","outputs":[{"internalType":"contract AggregatorV3Interface","name":"sequencerUptimeFeed","type":"address"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"getUptimeResyncWindow","outputs":[{"internalType":"uint256","name":"uptimeResyncWindow","type":"uint256"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"details":"Optimistic rollups (e.g., Arbitrum, Optimism) and many ZK-rollups rely on sequencers to efficiently manage transaction ordering, execution, and batching before submitting them to Layer 1. If this sequencer goes down, L2 applications will not be updated in a timely fashion, which is particularly important for price oracles. To mitigate this risk, Chainlink provides a sequencer uptime feed to track the status of the sequencer, including the time since the last restart (see https://docs.chain.link/data-feeds/l2-sequencer-feeds).","errors":{"SequencerResyncIncomplete()":[{"details":"Since outages result in a queue of delayed transactions which must be processed, the stabilization period (or resync window) allows sufficient time for the L2 network to \"catch up,\" so that the price feed is accurate."}]},"kind":"dev","methods":{"getSequencerUptimeFeed()":{"details":"This only applies to L2 networks that provide this feed to check on the status of the sequencer.","returns":{"sequencerUptimeFeed":"The address of the feed contract (or zero if L1 or unsupported)"}},"getUptimeResyncWindow()":{"details":"After an outage, the sequencer requires some time to \"catch up\" and ensure the L2 state matches L1.","returns":{"uptimeResyncWindow":"The length of the uptime resync window, in seconds"}}},"version":1},"userdoc":{"errors":{"SequencerDown()":[{"notice":"The uptime sequencer has returned a status of \"down\"."}],"SequencerResyncIncomplete()":[{"notice":"A price feed was accessed while still within the resync window (e.g., after a sequencer outage)."}]},"kind":"user","methods":{"getSequencerUptimeFeed()":{"notice":"Return the address of the sequencer uptime feed."},"getUptimeResyncWindow()":{"notice":"Return the length of the sequencer uptime resync window."}},"notice":"Interface to the LP oracle's sequencer uptime feed, used by L2s to indicate the L1 synchronization getSequencerUptimeFeed().details: This only applies to L2 networks that provide this feed to check on the status of the sequencer.

returns.sequencerUptimeFeed: The address of the feed contract (or zero if L1 or unsupported)

getUptimeResyncWindow().details: After an outage, the sequencer requires some time to "catch up" and ensure the L2 state matches L1.

returns.uptimeResyncWindow: The length of the uptime resync window, in seconds

getSequencerUptimeFeed().notice: Return the address of the sequencer uptime feed.

getUptimeResyncWindow().notice: Return the length of the sequencer uptime resync window.
